name: Indian IT Sector (TCS/Infosys/Wipro)
summary: Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers to 3 June morning). **THE RUPEE-REVENUE TAILWIND FLIPPED TO A HEADWIND — A NEW US TARIFF VECTOR DROVE AN 'IT STOCK SHOCK' COMPONENT OF THE 2-3 JUNE INDIA SELLOFF.** The defining new development for the sector is the USTR-proposed 12.5% Section 301 tariff on India (vs 10% for EU/UK/Canada/Mexico) — surfacing as a named driver of the 2 June rupee/Nifty weakness (The Week + Bloomberg). For Indian IT specifically, the tariff threat + a renewed US-demand-risk frame produced an 'IT stock shock' (markets C4/A7) — inverting the prior 'rupee weakness = exporter tailwind' read that had supported IT through May. The structural cycle-bear case is unchanged and now compounded: (1) AI flows continue to bypass Indian services for the Korea/Taiwan/US tech stack (SMH +26.1% MoM, KOSPI +31.5% MoM); (2) a US tariff/earnings-risk overhang on top. India is seeking a bilateral resolution on the tariff, but until resolved it is a non-oil, non-crisis headwind that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. The profit-warning trigger stays watching (no formal warning yet — the move is price/tariff-driven, not a company guidance cut). PRIOR — Day 78. **B7 INDIAN IT-PACK STRENGTH IS THE CYCLE-INTERNAL ROTATION SIGNAL — Infosys +1.92%, Tech Mahindra +1.79%** among the top gainers when Sensex closed May 15 -0.21% / Nifty -0.19%. **Interpretation**: US recession risk priced through Indian IT services (rupee-revenue tailwind from USD/INR ₹95.96 + flight-to-defensive within Indian equity bucket). This is the FIRST cycle session where Indian IT outperformed in a global stagflation-shock session — possibly the first sign of the AI-cycle-decoupling-from-India headwind beginning to invert, but more likely a one-session rupee-revenue defensive bid. **Nifty Pharma defensive bid intact; Nifty IT remains -3% YTD vs Nifty -14% YTD = the cycle-bear case on Indian IT is structural (US AI-pricing-pressure + AI-cycle bypassing Indian services), but the May 15 session is an internal-rotation signal worth tracking.** SMH $556.34 -3.8% 1D — Indian IT diverged from US tech selloff on the day, which is unusual and supports the rupee-revenue-defensive read. NVDA earnings May 20 next major catalyst. PRIOR (May 7): **AI-CYCLE DECOUPLING FROM INDIA STRUCTURALLY HARDENS — KOSPI +37.3% / TAIEX +26.2% / SMH +37.5% (1M) WHILE NIFTY UNDERPERFORMS RISK-ON.** Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production talks (Bloomberg May 5, CNBC corroborated) extend halo to Korea/Taiwan/US tech stack, NOT to Indian IT services. Samsung Q1 net profits 57.2T KRW (~$39B) — single quarter higher than ALL of 2025 combined; Samsung market cap crossed $1T May 6 on KOSPI 7,400 (+6.7%). Nikkei +5.7% Golden Week catch-up (SoftBank +13-16%, Advantest +8%, Tokyo Electron +9%) is international validation of AI-cycle decoupling. **For India deployment thesis:** AI flows continue to bypass India IT services and rotate to Korea/Taiwan/US tech — Indian-equity allocation continues to underperform structurally in this regime. Patel/SemiAnalysis: May 4-6 sequence is structural confirmation; Apple-Samsung-Intel talks specifically signal a US-AI-manufacturing-onshore tailwind that will rotate flows further from Indian IT services. Q4 FY26 earnings weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro, HCL Tech) remains dominant DOMESTIC driver. The peace-dividend session today did NOT lift Indian IT — direct-vs-indirect bifurcation persists.

active_triggers.condition: Major Indian IT company issues profit warning citing US demand slowdown

The structural bear (AI flows bypassing Indian services for the Korea/Taiwan/US stack) now carries a tariff/earnings overhang on top. India is seeking bilateral resolution; until then it does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. Profit-warning trigger stays watching (price/tariff-driven, not a company guidance cut).

recent_signals.verification: 12.5% US tariff on India = CONFIRMED (2 sources); 'IT stock shock' as a component of the India selloff = REPORTED/analytical (markets C4); rupee-revenue tailwind inversion = analytical read. No company profit warning yet — trigger stays watching.

recent_signals.headline: **INDIAN IT BENEFITS FROM RUPEE WEAKNESS — TCS, INFOSYS, HCL, TECH MAHINDRA, ORACLE, PERSISTENT UP 3.4-4.5% INTRADAY ON ₹96.44 + POSTPONEMENT RELIEF.** Bharti Airtel +1.66%. **Mechanism**: USD-denominated revenue translates better at weaker rupee — IT exporter idiosyncratic positive within structurally bearish India tape. **Classic 'good news for indices, bad news for the underlying economy' Indian-market schizophrenia** — IT exporters catch the postponement-relief while fuel pass-through fires and rupee weakens to fresh record low.
recent_signals.verification: IT stocks +3-4.5% intraday = REPORTED (TradingEconomics single-source); rupee-IT-exporter mechanism = CONFIRMED structural.
